personalInfo.name: Maria Garcia
personalInfo.title: PhD Student in Computer Science
personalInfo.tagline: Researching explainable AI and fairness in machine learning
personalInfo.bio: PhD student in Computer Science at Stanford University, advised by Prof. Jane Smith. My research focuses on developing interpretable machine learning models that are fair and robust across diverse populations.

elements.welcomeMessage: I'm a PhD student passionate about making AI systems more transparent, fair, and trustworthy. My research bridges machine learning, ethics, and human-computer interaction.
highlights: PhD Student at Stanford University (3rd year)
4 publications in top-tier venues (FAccT, CHI, NeurIPS)
Organizing committee member for AI Ethics Workshop

elements.title: Measuring and Mitigating Bias in Facial Recognition Systems
elements.description: Developed novel techniques to detect and reduce demographic bias in facial recognition models, improving fairness across age, gender, and ethnicity.

elements.title: Explainable AI for Medical Diagnosis: A User Study
elements.description: Conducted user studies with medical professionals to evaluate interpretability techniques for AI-assisted diagnosis systems.

elements.title: FairML: A Framework for Auditing Machine Learning Models
elements.description: Created open-source framework for systematic fairness auditing of ML models, now used by 50+ organizations.

elements.title: Survey: Fairness Definitions in Machine Learning
elements.description: Comprehensive survey of 40+ fairness definitions in ML literature, identifying gaps and future research directions.

teaching.name: Teaching
teaching.philosophy: I believe in creating inclusive learning environments where every student feels empowered to ask questions and explore ideas. My teaching emphasizes hands-on projects and real-world applications.

elements.description: Teaching Assistant for graduate-level machine learning course. Held weekly office hours, graded assignments, and led discussion sections on fairness and ethics in ML.
Teaching Assistant for introductory programming course in Python. Created supplementary materials for students from non-CS backgrounds.
Teaching Assistant for NLP course. Led project mentoring sessions and developed fairness evaluation framework for student projects.
